policy.scope: Curated index of authoritative, publicly accessible Raspberry Pi and component resources.
policy.copyright: Publicly accessible does not mean public domain. This catalog links to publisher-hosted material and does not redistribute third-party documents.
policy.freshness: Verify the linked source, board revision, module marking, and document revision before purchasing parts or applying power.

resources.title: Raspberry Pi computer hardware catalog
resources.summary: Official comparison tables for the complete Raspberry Pi computer range, including flagship, keyboard, Zero, Compute Module, Pico, legacy, and end-of-life models.
resources.license: CC BY-SA 4.0 documentation; linked, not redistributed

resources.title: Raspberry Pi Product Information Portal
resources.summary: Official portal for product briefs, mechanical drawings, compliance documents, notices, and other controlled product information.
resources.license: Publisher copyright and per-document terms; linked, not redistributed

resources.title: Raspberry Pi 5
resources.summary: Current high-performance flagship SBC with PCIe, dual MIPI camera/display connectors, and the standard 40-pin header.
resources.license: Publisher copyright; linked, not redistributed

watchouts: Use suitable cooling for sustained load
Use the official power guidance when powering USB and PCIe peripherals

resources.title: Raspberry Pi 4 Model B
resources.summary: Mature general-purpose SBC with broad accessory and software support.
resources.license: Publisher copyright; linked, not redistributed

watchouts: No exposed PCIe connector
Camera/display cable differs from Raspberry Pi 5

resources.title: Raspberry Pi 3 family
resources.summary: Official hardware documentation for Raspberry Pi 3 Model B, B+, and A+ variants, useful for maintaining existing builds.
resources.license: CC BY-SA 4.0 documentation; linked, not redistributed

watchouts: Confirm the exact model and revision
Use current lifecycle guidance before selecting for a new long-lived design

resources.title: Raspberry Pi Zero 2 W
resources.summary: Compact wireless Linux board for space- and power-constrained projects.
resources.license: Publisher copyright; linked, not redistributed

bestFor: camera projects with modest compute needs
watchouts: Header may require soldering
Mini HDMI and compact camera connector require the correct cables
Limited memory compared with flagship boards

resources.title: Raspberry Pi Zero family
resources.summary: Official model and connector comparison for all Zero variants, including wireless and pre-soldered-header distinctions.
resources.license: CC BY-SA 4.0 documentation; linked, not redistributed

resources.title: Raspberry Pi 500+
resources.summary: Keyboard computer for an integrated desktop and maker workstation, with internal SSD storage.
resources.license: Publisher copyright; linked, not redistributed

watchouts: Not suitable when the bare-board form factor is required
Check HAT mechanical access and cable routing

resources.title: Raspberry Pi 500
resources.summary: Integrated Raspberry Pi 5-generation keyboard computer for desktop, education, and bench use.
resources.license: Publisher copyright; linked, not redistributed

watchouts: Not a bare-board form factor
Check access before selecting full-size HATs

resources.title: Raspberry Pi 400
resources.summary: Raspberry Pi 4-generation keyboard computer with exposed GPIO for accessible desktop making.
resources.license: Publisher copyright; linked, not redistributed

watchouts: Use ribbon cables or extension hardware where direct HAT mounting is impractical

resources.title: Compute Module 5
resources.summary: Raspberry Pi 5-generation system-on-module for embedded products and custom carrier boards.
resources.license: Publisher copyright; linked, not redistributed

watchouts: Requires a compatible IO or custom carrier board
Carrier design needs controlled documentation and compliance review

resources.title: Compute Module 4 and 4S
resources.summary: Mature Compute Module generation with dual-connector CM4 and SODIMM-form-factor CM4S variants.
resources.license: CC BY-SA 4.0 documentation; linked, not redistributed

profile.formFactor: CM4 dual connectors or CM4S SODIMM
watchouts: CM4 and CM4S are mechanically different
Confirm exact memory, storage, and wireless ordering code

resources.title: Compute Module hardware and IO board compatibility
resources.summary: Official model, IO-board compatibility, flashing, boot, camera/display, and design-resource documentation for every Compute Module generation.
resources.license: CC BY-SA 4.0 documentation; linked, not redistributed

resources.title: Raspberry Pi Pico 2 and Pico 2 W
resources.summary: RP2350 microcontroller boards for deterministic control; W variants add wireless connectivity.
resources.license: Publisher copyright; linked, not redistributed

profile.header: Two 20-pin footprints; header depends on variant
watchouts: Does not run Raspberry Pi OS
3.3V GPIO
Confirm wireless and pre-soldered-header variant

resources.title: Raspberry Pi Pico and Pico W
resources.summary: Mature RP2040 microcontroller board family with extensive examples and ecosystem support.
resources.license: Publisher copyright; linked, not redistributed

profile.header: Two 20-pin footprints; header depends on variant
watchouts: Does not run Raspberry Pi OS
3.3V GPIO
Confirm W/H variant requirements

resources.license: Project documentation terms apply; linked, not redistributed

resources.title: Pico C/C++ SDK documentation
resources.summary: Official SDK and hardware API documentation for RP-series microcontrollers.
resources.license: CC BY-SA 4.0 documentation; linked, not redistributed

resources.title: MicroPython on Pico-series boards
resources.summary: Official MicroPython setup and workflow guidance for Pico-series boards.
resources.license: CC BY-SA 4.0 documentation; linked, not redistributed

resources.title: Raspberry Pi AI software
resources.summary: Official current prerequisites and workflows for Hailo-powered vision and generative AI on Raspberry Pi 5.
resources.license: CC BY-SA 4.0 documentation; linked, not redistributed

resources.title: Bosch BME280 environmental sensor
resources.summary: Popular digital environmental sensor. Confirm breakout-board regulator and level-shifting details in addition to the bare-sensor datasheet.
resources.license: Manufacturer copyright; linked, not redistributed

resources.title: DS18B20 1-Wire temperature sensor
resources.summary: Popular addressable temperature sensor; consult the manufacturer datasheet for pull-up, conversion-time, and parasite-power constraints.
resources.license: Manufacturer copyright; linked, not redistributed
resources.safety: Verify waterproof-probe wire colours; clone cable colour conventions are inconsistent.

resources.title: DHT22 / AM2302 humidity and temperature modules
resources.summary: Common low-cost humidity/temperature family. Module pinouts and required pull-ups vary, so identify the exact manufacturer and breakout before wiring.
resources.license: Publisher terms apply; linked, not redistributed
resources.safety: Do not infer a bare-sensor pinout from a three-pin breakout or vice versa.

resources.title: HC-SR04 ultrasonic distance module
resources.summary: Ubiquitous ultrasonic module sold by many vendors; use a supplier guide for the exact module and verify timings empirically.
resources.license: Publisher terms apply; linked, not redistributed
resources.safety: Typical Echo output is 5V; use a divider or level shifter before Raspberry Pi GPIO.

resources.title: MCP3008 8-channel ADC
resources.summary: Popular SPI analog-to-digital converter for adding analog inputs to Raspberry Pi computers.
resources.license: Manufacturer copyright; linked, not redistributed
resources.safety: Keep analog and digital input voltages within the selected supply and reference limits.

resources.title: ADS1115 16-bit ADC
resources.summary: Common I2C ADC for higher-resolution and differential measurements.
resources.license: Manufacturer copyright; linked, not redistributed
resources.safety: The programmable gain amplifier does not make inputs tolerant beyond the supply rails.

resources.title: PCA9685 PWM controller
resources.summary: Popular 16-channel I2C PWM controller used on servo and LED driver breakouts.
resources.license: Manufacturer copyright; linked, not redistributed
resources.safety: Power servos from a suitable external rail, not from GPIO; share ground.

resources.title: SSD1306 OLED display controller
resources.summary: Common monochrome OLED controller. Breakout resolution, interface straps, I2C address, and reset wiring vary.
resources.license: Manufacturer copyright; linked, not redistributed
resources.safety: Identify the breakout voltage and interface configuration before wiring.

resources.title: WS2812 / NeoPixel addressable LEDs
resources.summary: Widely used addressable RGB LEDs; use a current power budget, bulk capacitor, data resistor, and appropriate logic-level strategy.
resources.license: Publisher terms apply; linked, not redistributed
resources.safety: Never power a strip from GPIO. Use an adequate fused supply, common ground, and level shifting when required.

resources.title: MPU-6050 6-axis IMU
resources.summary: Popular legacy IMU found on many GY-521-style modules; module regulators, address straps, and clones vary.
resources.license: Manufacturer copyright; linked, not redistributed
resources.safety: Verify the exact module and logic voltage; do not assume every GY-521 clone is identical.

resources.title: INA219 current and power monitor
resources.summary: Common I2C high-side current and bus-voltage monitor.
resources.license: Manufacturer copyright; linked, not redistributed
resources.safety: Respect common-mode, shunt, package, and breakout trace limits; it is not an isolation device.

resources.title: VL53L0X time-of-flight distance sensor
resources.summary: Compact I2C time-of-flight ranging sensor used on many breakouts.
resources.license: Manufacturer copyright; linked, not redistributed
resources.safety: Confirm breakout voltage regulation and XSHUT/address setup when using multiple sensors.

resources.title: PN532 NFC controller
resources.summary: Popular legacy NFC controller available on I2C, SPI, and UART breakouts; check current product status before selecting it for a new design.
resources.license: Manufacturer copyright; linked, not redistributed
resources.safety: Check breakout interface switches, voltage translation, and antenna placement.

resources.title: MFRC522 RFID reader IC
resources.summary: Common 13.56 MHz reader IC found on low-cost RC522 modules. The manufacturer marks it end-of-life and not recommended for new designs, but its modules remain common in existing hobby projects.
resources.license: Manufacturer copyright; linked, not redistributed
resources.safety: Use 3.3V power and logic unless the exact breakout documentation explicitly says otherwise.

resources.title: TB6612FNG dual motor driver
resources.summary: Efficient dual H-bridge driver commonly used for small DC motors.
resources.license: Manufacturer copyright; linked, not redistributed
resources.safety: Use a suitable motor supply, bulk decoupling, conservative current limits, and common ground.

resources.title: L298 dual full-bridge driver
resources.summary: Common older bipolar motor driver. It is easy to source but has a substantial voltage drop and heat loss compared with modern MOSFET drivers.
resources.license: Manufacturer copyright; linked, not redistributed
resources.safety: Do not drive motors from GPIO or Pi power. Check module diode, regulator-jumper, heat, and supply details.

resources.title: ULN2003A Darlington driver array
resources.summary: Seven-channel sink driver with clamp diodes, often paired with relays and 28BYJ-48 steppers.
resources.license: Manufacturer copyright; linked, not redistributed
resources.safety: Use an external load supply and verify current, dissipation, and common-ground requirements.

resources.title: 74HC595 serial-in shift register
resources.summary: Popular serial-in/parallel-out logic device for modest digital-output expansion.
resources.license: Manufacturer copyright; linked, not redistributed
resources.safety: Observe per-pin and package current limits; use transistor or driver stages for larger loads.

resources.title: systemd service documentation
resources.summary: Upstream service-manager documentation for turning a proven foreground process into a supervised service.
resources.license: Project documentation terms apply; linked, not redistributed

resources.title: Raspberry Pi documentation source repository
resources.summary: Public source for official documentation, useful for checking changes, reporting errors, and understanding documentation licensing.
resources.license: Repository and documentation license terms apply
